LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF JAN TOLU'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE COURT TAKES THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. SEE STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND V. SUP. CRT. (2010) 184 CAL.APP.4TH 1124, 1130-1131 (AMENDED COMPLAINT MOOTS PENDING SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION). THE COURT CONTINUES THE TRIAL DATE TO DECEMBER 7, 2015, TO INSURE THAT NEEDED DISCOVERY CAN GO FORWARD AND TO THAT DEFENDANT CAN RENEW ITS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IF NECESSARY. THE CASE IS STILL IN ITS EARLY STAGES AND THIS IS THE FIRST TRIAL CONTINUANCE. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Jan Tolu For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-01-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF JAN TOLU'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted. The Court takes the motion for summary judgment as to plaintiff's complaint off calendar as moot. See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131 (amended complaint moots pending summary judgment motion). The court continues the trial date to December 7, 2015, to insure that needed discovery can go forward and to that defendant can renew its motion for summary judgment if necessary. The case is still in its early stages and this is the first trial continuance. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication (As To Jan Tolu)
Off Calendar Jun-01-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: As to DEFENDANT THE CELLAR NIGHTCLUB, LLC, AS SUCCESSOR TO LELAND VENTURES, LLC DBA THE CELLAR'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication As To Jan Tolu, the court grants plaintiff's motion to amend the complaint and takes the motion for summary judgment as to plaintiff's complaint off calendar as moot. See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131 (amended complaint moots pending summary judgment motion). The court continues the trial date to December 7, 2015, to insure that needed discovery can go forward and defendant can renew its motion for summary judgment if necessary. The case is still in its early stages and this is the first trial continuance.

The court denies defendant's motion for summary judgment/adjudication on the cross complaint. Defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production and the motion is premature. Liability has not been established and there is no judgment. "The obligation of indemnity [is] "the obligation resting on one party to make good a loss or damage another has incurred." E.E. White, Inc. v. City of Huntington Beach (1978) 21 Cal.3d 497, 506; see also Herrero v. Atkinson (1964) 227 Cal.App.2d 69, 74 ("The duty to indemnify may arise, and indemnity may be allowed in those fact situations where in equity and good conscience the burden of the judgment should be shifted from the shoulders of the person seeking indemnity to the one from whom indemnity is sought.") (emphasis added). The motion does not
comply with the CRC. The notice of motion and separate statement are not entirely clear exactly what should be adjudicated. The notice of motion refers to adjudicating the causes of action, but it also seeks a determination that defendant is not at fault and SIS must defend the action. The separate statement does not comply with CRC 3.1350. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
